author: Shibli Numani, Maulana
title: Swanih Zebun Nisa Begam
gloss: A brief sketch of the life of the Princess Zebun Nisa, daughter, of the Emperor Aurangzeb.
notes: By Shibli Numani, the major Urdu historian-biographer. Date given only as June 1910.

author: Shiv Biarat Lal, Babu, M. A.
title: Hamari Mataen
gloss: A book giving a brief description of the lives of a few noted women of ancient India such as Bimla, Chandar Kanta, Dropdi, Sita, Tara Mati, Parvati, etc., reprinted from the Sadhu Magazine of Lahore.
notes: 'Our Mothers' — ideal-womanhood compendium. [Previous edition noted in entry No. 9 on page 45 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th September, 1908.] End of URDU—BIOGRAPHY (serials 30–39 this quarter).

author: Ahsan, M.
title: Dilfarosh
gloss: For subject see entry No. 1 on page 26 of of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1909.
notes: Cross-reference: 1909Q2 entry 1, page 26. Dilfarosh is Mehdi Hasan Ahsan's Urdu adaptation of The Merchant of Venice. Catalogue misprints date as '19.0'.

author: Ali Bakhsh, Raja
title: Suwang Bakawali
gloss: The old story of Bakawali dramatised.
copyright: Sayad Maqbul Husaine, Rewári; No. 115, dated the 20th July, 1910.
notes: Swang (folk-theatre) text from Rewari, outside Punjab proper — registered in Punjab.

author: Ghanaya Lal, L.
title: Gain Tamasha-i-Fateh Jang
gloss: Songs from the drama Fateh Jang.
notes: Publisher of serial 6 appearing here as author.

author: Ghulam Ali, Diwana, Munshi
title: Mukammil Drama Mihr Jiya
gloss: A drama in which Indar Singh, the father of the heroine, having spent money lavishly on the marriage of her sister, is obliged to marry the heroine to an old man.
publisher: Bhai Dayal Singh and Sons, Book-sellers
notes: Date given only as June 1910. Social drama on marriage expenses and mismatched marriage.

author: Ghulam Ali, Diwana, Munshi
title: Taid-i-Yazdani
gloss: The story of a prince whose uncles expels him, but who succeeded in getting back his kingdom after many trials.
publisher: Bhai Daya Singh and Sons, Book-sellers
copyright: B. Daya Singh, Book-seller, Lahore; No. 110, dated the 5th September, 1910.
notes: Author dash-continuation of serial 9. Sikh book-seller holding copyright of Muslim playwright's drama.
